models.generation_context: 2nd-gen (BK), introduced 2018; refreshed 2022; MY2026 adds Prestige Graphite trim and broader 10.25" navigation availability
models.model_summary: Compact luxury sport sedan offered with turbo I4 and twin-turbo V6 powertrains, rear- or all-wheel drive, and standard 8-speed automatic. For 2026 Genesis adds a Prestige Graphite trim based on the 3.3T, expands standard 10.25" navigation, and refreshes wheel designs.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 VDS score for G70. Consumer Reports has rated recent G70 model years (2023, 2025) as less reliable than other cars of the same model year; the 2026 model receives only minor changes from those generations.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 G70-specific APEAL score. Genesis as a brand has historically ranked at or near the top of premium-brand APEAL studies.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the G70's sharp driving dynamics, willing twin-turbo V6, and luxurious interior for the price; common critiques are tight rear-seat and trunk space and the aging infotainment vs European rivals. The Prestige Graphite trim has won a Car of Texas award from the Texas Auto Writers Association and the G70 is a 2026 CarGurus Best Smart Luxury Vehicle.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review aggregates for the 2026 G70 are still thin given recent-MY release. Prior-MY owners cite engaging handling and luxurious cabin as the most-mentioned positives, with rear-seat space and infotainment lag as recurring complaints.

trims.notes: Base trim of the G70's sole powertrain line (2.5T). The 3.3T trims are separate powertrain-line bases. NHTSA has not published a current-MY G70 overall rating (fetch returned 403 during research); IIHS 2026 G70 page shows Good crashworthiness scores but no TSP/TSP+ designation per the IIHS 2025 awards roster. Curb weight estimated mid-band from manufacturer 3,836-4,063 lb range cited in retailer spec docs.

notable_other: Heated steering wheel added vs RWD

changes: EPA combined MPG drops to 23 (vs 24 RWD)
Adds Nappa leather upholstery (vs synthetic leather)
Adds 12-way power driver seat with memory
Adds Surround View Monitor and Blind-Spot View Monitor
Adds 12-way power driver seat with memory
Adds Head-Up Display, Surround View Monitor, Blind-Spot View Monitor

trims.notes: Base trim of the G70's 3.3T powertrain line; is_base_trim true and delta_from_base null per sole-trim atomic rule within the 3.3T line. EPA ID 49253 verified as 2026 G70 RWD 3.3L Turbo V6.

changes: EPA combined MPG drops to 19 (vs 20 RWD)

notable_other: Exclusive Ceres Blue or Vatna Gray exterior
Ultramarine Blue Nappa leather with orange contrast stitching

trims.notes: New for MY2026. Base trim of its own trim_family (Prestige Graphite); is_base_trim true and delta_from_base null per sole-trim atomic rule (singleton trim_family). Cited Genesis newsroom press release for trim-specific features and MSRP.

changes: EPA combined MPG drops to 19 (vs 20 RWD)

models.notes: Two powertrain lines (2.5T and 3.3T) each have their own base trim per the multi-powertrain rule. Prestige Graphite is split into its own trim_family because it has distinct exclusive paint colors and interior treatment per Genesis press release. IIHS 2026 page shows individual rating components but G70 is NOT on the 2025 IIHS TSP/TSP+ list for Genesis (per Genesis newsroom 2025 IIHS announcement). NHTSA has not published 2026 G70 overall stars; older 2019 small overlap rating still cited on IIHS page. Genesis G70 was named CarGurus 2026 Best Smart Luxury Vehicle and Prestige Graphite won Texas Auto Writers Association 2026 Car of Texas.

models.generation_context: RG3 generation, introduced 2021; refreshed for MY2026 with restyled exterior, new 27" OLED display, and AWD made standard across the lineup
models.model_summary: Midsize luxury sedan with standard AWD across all trims for 2026. Offered with turbocharged 2.5L I4 (300 hp) or twin-turbo 3.5L V6 (375 hp). The Electrified G80 plug-in hybrid variant has been discontinued in the US for MY2026; G80 is now ICE-only.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 G80 VDS score. Consumer Reports does not currently publish a numerical predicted-reliability score for the 2026 G80; older G80 model years receive mixed reliability ratings.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 G80-specific APEAL score. Genesis brand consistently ranks at the top of the premium APEAL rankings.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the refreshed 2026 G80's elegant styling, lush interior with new 27-inch OLED display, smooth ride, and class-leading value relative to German competitors; common critiques are reduced rear-cargo flexibility versus rivals and the relative lack of dynamic sharpness in the V6 variant.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner aggregates for 2026 G80 are still developing. Prior-MY G80 owners commonly cite quiet ride, value, and design as strengths and report some software/infotainment quirks as common complaints.

notable_other: Standard AWD across the lineup for 2026

trims.notes: Base trim of G80's 2.5T powertrain line. For 2026 Genesis dropped RWD across G80 and Electrified G80 entirely; AWD is now standard. EPA ID 49312 verified as 2026 G80 AWD 2.5L Turbo I4.

changes: Adds Nappa leather upholstery (vs synthetic leather)
Adds ventilated front seats and heated rear seats
Adds adaptive dampers with Road Preview

trims.notes: Base trim of the G80's 3.5T powertrain line per multi-powertrain rule. EPA ID 49313 verified as 2026 G80 AWD 3.5L Turbo V6.

notable_other: Open-pore Real Black Ash Wood trim
Replaces prior Sport Advanced trim for MY2026

trims.notes: New for MY2026, replaces the prior Sport Advanced trim. Singleton trim_family (Prestige Black gets its own family because of all-black exterior/interior styling); is_base_trim true and delta_from_base null per sole-trim atomic rule. Press-release image scraping needed since trim-specific images aren't directly extractable from gallery.

models.notes: MY2026 G80 has TWO powertrain lines (2.5T and 3.5T) with the Prestige Black trim acting as its own family because of distinct all-black styling treatment. AWD made standard across the entire G80 lineup for 2026 (RWD dropped). Electrified G80 (PHEV) sedan discontinued in the US for MY2026 per multiple confirmations (although EPA published a 2026 Electrified G80 entry, Genesis removed it from the consumer lineup and Newsroom announced it is no longer offered in the US). IIHS 2026 G80 4-door sedan: TSP+ awarded but only for vehicles built after June 2025. Cargo trunk: 13.1 cu ft per Genesis spec page.

models.generation_context: RS4 (5th-gen) G90, introduced for 2023; E-Supercharger 48V mild-hybrid variant added 2023 and continues for 2026
models.model_summary: Full-size flagship luxury sedan with twin-turbo 3.5L V6, standard AWD, and an available 48V e-Supercharger mild-hybrid variant that bumps output to 409 hp. Offered in three trims, all with three-zone climate, panoramic roof, and 23-speaker Bang & Olufsen audio.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 G90 VDS score. Consumer Reports' G90 reliability data is sparse given low sales volume. Genesis brand placed 3rd in JD Power 2025 VDS overall premium-brand rankings.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a model-specific 2026 G90 APEAL score. Genesis brand consistently ranks at or near the top of the premium APEAL studies.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the G90's serene cabin, advanced 27-inch OLED display, optional E-Supercharger powertrain's lag-free response, and remarkable price-to-luxury value relative to S-Class and 7-Series; common critiques are uninspired exterior styling versus rivals and the chassis tuning emphasizing isolation over engagement.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner aggregates for the 2026 G90 are limited given its niche positioning and low sales volume. Earlier-MY G90 owners commonly praise interior comfort and quietness; some report software/infotainment lag.

trims.notes: Base trim of the G90's non-MHEV powertrain line. EPA ID 49314 verified as 2026 G90 AWD 3.5L Turbo V6 (non-MHEV). IIHS 2026 G90 page shows TSP award (not TSP+) with most ratings applying to 2023-26 models.

notable_other: Multi-chamber air suspension with rear-wheel steering

trims.notes: Base trim of the G90's MHEV (E-Supercharger) powertrain line per multi-powertrain rule; is_base_trim true and delta_from_base null per sole-trim atomic rule for that line. Powertrain type classified as 'hybrid' (mild hybrid 48V) per spec taxonomy. EPA ID 49828 verified as 2026 G90 MHEV. Battery capacity 0.27 kWh estimated for 48V MHEV system (Genesis does not publish exact figure).

notable_other: Includes all 3.5T E-Supercharger features (air suspension, rear-wheel steering, executive rear seats)

trims.notes: Singleton trim_family (Prestige Black gets its own family because of all-black exterior/interior treatment); is_base_trim true and delta_from_base null per sole-trim atomic rule. Trim-specific images need follow-up scraping from genesis.com page.

models.notes: Two main powertrain lines: 3.5T (non-MHEV, 375 hp) and 3.5T E-Supercharger (48V MHEV, 409 hp). The Prestige Black is its own trim_family because of the distinct all-black styling treatment. IIHS shows the G90 with Top Safety Pick (not TSP+) per IIHS 2025 awards. Battery capacity for the 48V MHEV system is an estimate; Genesis does not publish the exact kWh figure. Pricing has been broadly reported as $92,700/$103,000/$105,750 (including destination); we encode msrp_base (excluding destination) and the $1,495 destination separately per schema convention.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen GV60, introduced 2022 (E-GMP platform shared with Hyundai Ioniq 5/Kia EV6); MY2026 adds native NACS port, upgraded 84 kWh long-range battery, and revised single-motor RWD entry trim
models.model_summary: Genesis' all-electric compact luxury SUV on Hyundai's E-GMP 800V platform. For 2026 Genesis adds a native NACS charging port (Tesla Supercharger access), upgrades to an 84 kWh battery for longer range, and introduces a new single-motor Standard RWD trim alongside the dual-motor AWD, Advanced AWD, and Performance AWD trims.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 GV60 VDS score. Consumer Reports has limited data on the GV60 given low volume; brand-wide Genesis ranked 3rd in 2025 JD Power VDS. EVs of this platform (Ioniq 5, EV6) have had some software recalls.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 GV60-specific APEAL score; GV60 was named US News & World Report Best Compact Electric SUV in the 2026 Best Hybrid and Electric Car Awards.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the GV60's quick acceleration (especially Performance trim's sub-4-second 0-60 with Boost Mode), opulent interior, 800V ultra-fast charging, and now NACS port for Tesla Supercharger access; common critiques are short range relative to competitors and tight rear cargo space versus larger EV SUVs.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner aggregates for the 2026 GV60 are limited due to its niche status. Earlier-MY owners praise straight-line speed and interior fit/finish; some report charging-network compatibility issues now addressed by the NACS port.

trims.notes: Sole base trim for the GV60 (EV-only model with one base for the entire family). For 2026 a new single-motor RWD entry trim is offered (225 hp). EPA ID 49652 verified as 2026 GV60 RWD. MPGe mirrored into city/highway/combined MPG per spec §3.6 v1.1 convention. Note that 'gv60-standard' trim_family includes RWD and AWD body-identical trims; the Performance trim sits in its own family because of its 21" wheels and visual differentiation.

changes: Increases output to 314 hp / 446 lb-ft (vs 225 hp / 258 lb-ft)
EPA range falls to 282 mi (vs 306 mi)

trims.notes: EPA ID 49653 verified as 2026 GV60 AWD with 19" wheels.

changes: Adds ventilated front seats and heated rear seats

trims.notes: EPA ID 49654 verified as 2026 GV60 AWD Advanced with 20" wheels.

notable_other: Dual-motor 429 hp / 516 lb-ft (483 hp with Boost Mode)

trims.notes: Singleton trim_family (Performance has distinct 21" wheels and visual differentiation including unique grille treatment and badging); is_base_trim true and delta_from_base null per sole-trim atomic rule. EPA ID 49655 verified as 2026 GV60 AWD Performance. Boost Mode briefly raises output to 483 hp for 10-second sprints.

models.notes: EV-only model; treated as a single powertrain line. New for 2026: native NACS port (replacing CCS1) gives Tesla Supercharger access, upgraded 84 kWh long-range battery boosts range, and a new single-motor RWD entry trim. Standard/Advanced trims share a trim_family; Performance is split into its own family because of unique 21" wheels and visual differentiation. GV60 won US News & World Report Best Compact Electric SUV in the 2026 Best Hybrid and Electric Car Awards.

models.generation_context: JK1 generation, introduced 2022; mid-cycle refresh for MY2026 brings new 27-inch OLED display, redesigned headlights and grille, updated wheels, and new climate-control interface. Won 2026 Wards 10 Best Interiors & UX award.
models.model_summary: Midsize luxury SUV with turbo 2.5L I4 (300 hp) or twin-turbo 3.5L V6 (375 hp); standard AWD. The 2026 refresh introduces a 27-inch OLED display spanning the dash, refreshed exterior styling, and updated interface. The 2026 GV70 was named a 2026 Wards 10 Best Interiors & UX winner and IIHS Top Safety Pick+.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 GV70 VDS score; earlier-MY GV70 (2023, 2024) was rated near-average for the brand. Genesis brand placed 3rd in 2025 JD Power premium-brand VDS.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 GV70-specific APEAL score. GV70 won 2026 Wards 10 Best Interiors & UX award.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the GV70's refreshed interior, expansive 27-inch OLED display, comfortable ride, and competitive value versus German rivals; common critiques are slightly tight cargo space versus larger competitors and the relative softness of the standard 2.5T's driving feel.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner aggregates for the refreshed 2026 GV70 are still developing. Earlier-MY owners cite interior quality, ride comfort, and value as commonly praised attributes; some report infotainment lag and tight rear cargo space as drawbacks.

trims.notes: Base trim of the GV70's 2.5T powertrain line; AWD standard across all trims. EPA ID 49483 verified as 2026 GV70 AWD (4 Mode, base setup without 5th drive mode). Press release pricing was $47,985 at launch but Genesis raised base MSRP to $48,985 mid-cycle. Wireless phone charging starts on 2.5T Select. Towing capacity per manufacturer 'properly equipped' rating.

changes: Adds ventilated front seats and heated rear seats
Adds wireless phone charging and panoramic sunroof

trims.notes: Sole trim of its Sport Prestige trim_family for the 2.5T line (sport styling with 21" wheels differentiates from non-Sport GV70 2.5T family); is_base_trim true and delta_from_base null per sole-trim atomic rule. EPA ID 49482 verified as 2026 GV70 AWD (5 Mode, the sport-styled 2.5T setup). Different fuel economy from base 2.5T due to wider/larger wheels. Singleton trim_family carries 2 of 4 required image angles (front_three_quarter, interior_dashboard); rear_three_quarter and side_profile not yet extracted from Genesis Scene7 CDN — manufacturer-press Sport Prestige photography set exists but per-angle Scene7 IDs not yet recorded. Image gap documented per spec §7 fallback.
Base trim of GV70's 3.5T Sport powertrain line per multi-powertrain rule. EPA ID 49484 verified as 2026 GV70 AWD 3.5L Turbo V6.

changes: Adds heated and ventilated rear seats

models.notes: Three trim_family groups: gv70-25t (standard styling for 2.5T base/Select/Advanced), gv70-25t-sport (Sport Prestige 2.5T with sport styling/21" wheels — singleton), gv70-35t-sport (3.5T Sport Advanced + Sport Prestige sharing sport body). The Electrified GV70 is treated as a separate model entry per genesis.com marketing. Major MY2026 refresh: new 27-inch OLED dash, redesigned headlights/grille, updated climate-control interface. Won 2026 Wards 10 Best Interiors & UX award. Destination fee $1,450 per Feb 2025 Genesis press release.

models.generation_context: EV variant of the GV70 (JK1 platform), introduced for MY2023. MY2026 adds native NACS charging port, larger 84 kWh battery, and tracks the ICE GV70's mid-cycle refresh styling.
models.model_summary: All-electric variant of the GV70, sharing the platform but with dual-motor AWD producing 429 hp (483 hp with Boost Mode) and 516 lb-ft. For 2026 a native NACS port replaces CCS1, the battery grows to 84 kWh, and range increases to 263 miles (base) or 243 miles (larger-wheel trims).

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Electrified GV70 VDS score; the model's volume is too low for distinct CR data. Genesis brand placed 3rd in 2025 JD Power premium-brand VDS.

customer_satisfaction.summary: No 2026 Electrified GV70-specific APEAL score from JD Power. The model is a 2025/2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Electrified GV70's combination of luxury and 429-hp dual-motor performance, the 800V architecture's fast charging, and the new-for-2026 NACS port; common critiques are shorter range than competing EV SUVs and a slightly cramped rear cargo area versus larger rivals.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner aggregates for the 2026 Electrified GV70 are still developing. Earlier-MY owners commonly praise straight-line acceleration and interior quality.

trims.notes: Base trim of Electrified GV70 (the model has only one powertrain configuration; all trims share dual-motor 429 hp AWD). EPA ID 49656 verified as 2026 Electrified GV70 AWD with 19-inch wheels. MPGe mirrored into MPG fields per spec §3.6 v1.1. IIHS rating shared with GV70 4-door SUV class. NACS port new for 2026.

changes: Adds Head-Up Display and Surround View Monitor
EPA range falls to 243 mi (vs 263 mi)

trims.notes: EPA ID 49657 verified as 2026 Electrified GV70 AWD with 20-inch wheels.

notable_other: Driver's seat with leg cushion extension

changes: Adds heated and ventilated rear seats

models.notes: EV-only model with one powertrain (dual-motor 429 hp). All three trims share one trim_family. For 2026 Genesis adds a native NACS port (new) and upgraded 84 kWh battery (up from 77.4 kWh on prior MY). Range varies by wheel size: 263 mi on 19" base, 243 mi on 20" Advanced/Prestige. IIHS 2025-26 Electrified GV70 holds TSP+ award per Genesis newsroom 2025 IIHS announcement.

models.generation_context: JX1 generation, introduced 2021; MY2026 adds a new base 2.5T RWD trim (-$2,150 vs prior AWD entry), revised lineup with up to 7 trims, and makes the 3rd row available on Advanced and Prestige trims with either engine. Mid-cycle refresh shares 27" OLED display introduction with G80/GV70.
models.model_summary: Three-row midsize luxury SUV. New for 2026 a 2.5T RWD entry trim joins the lineup at $57,700. Available with turbocharged 2.5L I4 (300 hp), twin-turbo 3.5L V6 (375 hp), or twin-turbo 3.5L V6 with 48V e-Supercharger (409 hp, Prestige Black). Available 7-seat third row offered on most trims.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 GV80 VDS score. Earlier-MY GV80 reliability has been rated near-average. Genesis brand placed 3rd in 2025 JD Power premium-brand VDS.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 GV80-specific APEAL score. GV80 has historically rated well in the segment.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the GV80's elegant design, hushed cabin, available third-row practicality, refined ride, and now a more accessible price-of-entry with the new 2.5T RWD; common critiques are reduced 3rd-row roominess versus segment leaders and the 2.5T's noticeable effort under heavy load.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner aggregates for the 2026 GV80 are still developing. Earlier-MY GV80 owners commonly praise interior comfort, design, and value; some report mediocre fuel economy and tight third-row legroom as drawbacks.

notable_other: 5-passenger 2-row configuration (3rd row not available on this trim)

trims.notes: New for MY2026. Base trim of GV80's 2.5T powertrain line. 5-passenger 2-row only (3rd row not available on base 2.5T per Genesis press info). EPA ID 49445 verified as 2026 GV80 RWD 2.5L Turbo I4. Towing 6,000 lb when properly equipped. Cargo behind 2nd row reflects 2-row config.
EPA ID 49577 verified as 2026 GV80 AWD 2.5L Turbo I4.

changes: Adds ventilated front seats and heated rear seats
Adds Head-Up Display, Surround View Monitor
Adds panoramic sunroof and Bang & Olufsen 18-speaker audio
Adds wireless phone charging and ventilated front seats

trims.notes: First trim to add the 3rd row (now available on Advanced trims). Capacity changes from 5 seats / 2 rows to 7 seats / 3 rows.
Base trim of the GV80's 3.5T non-MHEV powertrain line per multi-powertrain rule. EPA ID 49578 verified as 2026 GV80 AWD 3.5L Turbo V6 (non-MHEV). Includes power-folding 3rd row as standard.

changes: Adds heated and ventilated rear seats

notable_other: Black Nappa leather interior with open-pore black ash wood trim

trims.notes: Singleton trim_family (Prestige Black with E-Supercharger MHEV powertrain and exclusive black styling). Is_base_trim true and delta_from_base null per sole-trim atomic rule (this trim is the base AND only trim of its powertrain line + family). EPA ID 49579 verified as 2026 GV80 MHEV. Battery capacity 0.27 kWh estimated for 48V MHEV system. Trim-specific images need follow-up scraping.

models.notes: Three powertrain lines: 2.5T (300 hp ICE), 3.5T (375 hp non-MHEV), and 3.5T E-Supercharger MHEV (409 hp, Prestige Black only). New 2.5T RWD entry trim added for 2026 (-$2,150 vs prior entry). Power-folding 3rd row available on Advanced and Prestige trims with either engine. IIHS 2026 GV80: Top Safety Pick+. Pricing has some variation across secondary sources (~$83,250-$85,395 for Prestige Black); we use Genesis-listed values cross-checked across recent dealer/news sources.

models.generation_context: Sloped-roof body-style variant of the GV80, introduced 2025 for MY2025. Shares GV80 platform; treated as a separate model per genesis.com marketing. Two-row 5-passenger configuration only.
models.model_summary: Five-passenger 2-row coupe-styled midsize luxury SUV based on the GV80. Three trims: 3.5T (375 hp non-MHEV), 3.5T e-Supercharger MHEV (409 hp), and 3.5T e-SC MHEV Prestige Black (409 hp with exclusive black styling and 22" wheels). All trims AWD-standard with twin-turbo 3.5L V6.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 GV80 Coupe VDS score. Genesis brand placed 3rd in 2025 JD Power premium-brand VDS.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 GV80 Coupe-specific APEAL score.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the GV80 Coupe's bold sloped-roof styling, plush interior, e-Supercharger powertrain's lag-free response, and value relative to BMW X6/Mercedes GLE Coupe; common critiques are reduced cargo and rear headroom versus the standard GV80 and the steep price-of-entry.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner aggregates for the GV80 Coupe are limited given its recent introduction and niche positioning. Early owners praise styling and powertrain refinement.

trims.notes: Base trim of GV80 Coupe's non-MHEV powertrain line. EPA does not have a separate GV80 Coupe listing for 2026; cites EPA ID 49578 (GV80 AWD 3.5L Turbo V6) since the Coupe shares the same powertrain. Genesis spec page does not separately publish MY26 GV80 Coupe MPG; values mirror GV80 3.5T. IIHS has not separately rated the GV80 Coupe 4-door SUV body for 2026.

Base trim of the GV80 Coupe's E-Supercharger MHEV powertrain line; is_base_trim true and delta_from_base null per sole-trim atomic rule (this trim is also the only e-Supercharger non-Prestige-Black trim, so it's sole trim of its family). EPA ID 49579 verified as 2026 GV80 MHEV (shared with standard GV80 Prestige Black). Battery capacity 0.27 kWh estimated for 48V MHEV system.

notable_other: Black Nappa leather interior with unique quilting pattern

trims.notes: Singleton trim_family (Prestige Black gets its own family because of exclusive all-black styling). Is_base_trim true and delta_from_base null per sole-trim atomic rule. EPA ID 49579 verified as 2026 GV80 MHEV. Battery capacity 0.27 kWh estimated for 48V MHEV system. Some trim-specific images need follow-up scraping.

models.notes: GV80 Coupe is split as a separate model from GV80 per genesis.com marketing (own showroom page) and distinct body-style with two-row 5-passenger configuration only (no 3rd row option). Two main powertrain lines: 3.5T non-MHEV (375 hp) and 3.5T e-Supercharger MHEV (409 hp). Prestige Black is its own trim_family because of distinct all-black styling and 22" dark-finish wheels. EPA does not maintain GV80 Coupe-specific fuel economy IDs for 2026 — Coupe powertrains share EPA IDs 49578/49579 with standard GV80. IIHS has not separately rated the GV80 Coupe body for 2026; safety inherits from GV80 platform's ADAS standard equipment. NHTSA has not separately rated the GV80 Coupe for 2026.
